title: Galactic Standard Calendar
content:
{{Top|canon=Galactic standard calendar}}
{{Quote|Born some nineteen years ago by the Galactic Standard Calendar.|[[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Birra Seah]], in her report on [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] agent [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]|<ref name="Star Wars (2013) 10">[[Star Wars (2013) 10|''Star Wars'' (2013) 10]]</ref>}}
The '''Galactic Standard Calendar''' was the standard dating system used throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. The most recent of these [['ABY-BBY' dating system/Legends|calendars]] eras used the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] as its epoch, or "year zero."

==History==
{{Quote|It was the Historical Council of the Galactic Federation of Free Alliances that established and popularized the Battle of Yavin as the zero point to mark the beginning of our current society. Despite protests from some younger generations who maintain that subsequent battles were more significant, most educated beings agree that the Battle of Yavin was a hugely pivotal moment in galactic history.|[[Tionne Solusar]]|<ref name="JvS">[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]</ref>}}
As galactic civilization had existed for more than 25,000 years, maintaining a unified and standardized calendar in the entire galaxy has always been extremely challenging.<ref name="TCSWE">{{CSWECite|I||A Timeline of Galactic Events}}</ref> Over time, however, historians have used numerous galaxy-changing events as epochs to mark new calendar eras.<ref name="reform">{{HoloNetNewsWeb|45|life/13228_2.html|RM&S; Debates Calendar Reform}}</ref>

One particularly notable epoch is the [[Treaty of Coruscant]] of [[3653 BBY]]. The calendar eras before and after this event (referred to as "BTC" and "ATC," respectively) were popularized by the famous Jedi historian [[Gnost-Dural]]. His holographic records, which used this numbering system, contained some of the most complete records of numerous important events such as the [[Hundred-Year Darkness/Legends|Hundred-Year Darkness]], the [[Great Hyperspace War/Legends|Great Hyperspace War]], the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]], the [[Mandalorian Wars]], the [[Jedi Civil War]], and the [[Great Galactic War]] against the returned Sith Empire. For this reason, this method of numbering years remains important to historians.<ref name="Timeline 1: Treaty of Coruscant>{{TORweb|url=holonet/galactic-history|text=Timeline 1: Treaty of Coruscant|int=Timeline 1: Treaty of Coruscant}} ''</ref> Millennia later, the [[Ruusan Reformations/Legends|Ruusan Reformation]] of [[1000 BBY/Legends|1000 BBY]] substantially restructured the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and created a new [[Unidentified calendar (Ruusan Reformation)|calendar]] with the year 1000 BBY as its zero point.<ref name="TNEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref>

Centuries later, in [[35 BBY/Legends|35 BBY]], the [[Republic Measures & Standards Bureau]] established the [[Great ReSynchronization]] [[Great ReSynchronization calendar|calendar]] to rebalance the disparate dating systems used by the Galactic Republic. In [[23 BBY/Legends|23 BBY]], the Republic Measures & Standards Bureau debated whether to keep this calendar as one of the twenty or so official calendars of the Republic, with [[Keelen Ma]] commenting that the various researchers were getting tired of needing calendar converters on their pads.<ref name="reform" /> The Great ReSynchronization calendar eventually emerged as the main dating system used in the galaxy during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] and the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Era]].<ref name="HN">[[HoloNet News (Insider)|''Holonet News'']]</ref><ref name="GN">''[[Galaxywide NewsNets]]''</ref>

Around [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Historical Council]] established its own [['ABY-BBY' dating system/Legends|dating system]]. From that point on, the year in which the Battle of Yavin occurred was the epoch used for the dating system.<ref name="TEC">[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']]</ref> The New Republic's calendar was still in use at the time of the [[Sith–Imperial War]].<ref name="TCSWE" />

==Definition==
A [[Standard year/Legends|standard year]], as defined by the [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]-based [[Galactic Standard Time/Legends|Galactic Standard Time]], counted 368 [[Standard day/Legends|standard days]] of 24 [[Standard hour/Legends|standard hours]]. One standard year included 10 [[Standard month/Legends|standard months]] + 3 festival weeks + 3 holidays. The duration of the standard month and year had been used originally to establish the Galactic Standard Calendar.<ref name="Revised">''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game, Second Edition, Revised and Expanded]]''</ref>

However, calendar reforms<ref name="reform" /> had eventually changed the Galactic Standard Calendar to a 12-month/368-day calendar.<ref name="MCBY">{{Blog|holocron|13/comments|Major Character Birth Years}}</ref><ref name="DRT">{{Blog|eeusuestornii|7|Don't Read This!!!!!!}}</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Although the standard year was originally defined as a 10-month year early in the [[Star Wars Legends|Expanded Universe]],<ref name="Revised" /> the keeper of the [[Holocron continuity database]] [[Leland Chee]] revealed in [[2005]] that the 10-month calendar system had been dropped for further publications and replaced by a more convenient 12-month calendar.<ref name="MCBY" /> The use of a 12-month/368-day calendar was later confirmed by [[Sue Rostoni]].<ref name="DRT" /> Thus, the reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' featured years of 12 months and months of 30 or 31 days.<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> However, [[Karen Traviss]] had claimed that she used the 10-month calendar when writing her ''[[Republic Commando]]'' novels<ref name="SWBoards 1">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=222689&start=1230#13478643|thread=Books, Comics, & Television VIPs|user=[[Karen Traviss]]|date=2005-12-03|time=12:48 PM}}</ref><ref name="SWBoards 2">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=222689&start=1365#13853393|thread=Books, Comics, & Television VIPs|user=[[Karen Traviss]]|date=2006-01-16|time=2:00 AM}}</ref>—despite the fact that, in said novels, the one-[[Standard year/Legends|year]] anniversary of the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|First Battle of Geonosis]] occurs approximately twelve months after the battle.<ref name="AnniversaryDates">Karen Traviss's novel ''[[Republic Commando: Hard Contact]]'' dates the [[Battle of Qiilura]] to three [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|First Battle of Geonosis]]. Furthermore, the events of the main section of ''Republic Commando: Triple Zero'', which take place nine months after the Battle of Qiilura, span twenty-eight days and begin on the [[Standard day/Legends|day]] before the one-[[Standard year/Legends|year]] anniversary of the First Battle of Geonosis. Therefore the one-year anniversary of the First Battle of Geonosis must have taken place twelve months after the battle.</ref>
